title: 锦州市第二学期期末考试真题卷

sections.title: 一、阅读理解 第一节 A
sections.instruction: 根据短文内容从各题的四个选项中选择一个最佳答案。
sections.passage:
Eat Well, Live Well
We eat food so that we have energy to run, jump and play. We should eat many different types of food.
Dairy products (乳制品)
We should eat many dairy products such as milk, cheese, yogurt and so on. Along with fish, beans, and meat, dairy products make us strong.
Grain products
We should also eat enough grain products so that we have energy to work and play. Grain products include (包含) bread, rice and noodles.
Fruit and vegetables
Fruit and vegetables keep us healthy. We should eat enough fruit and vegetables at every meal. Try to eat fruit and vegetables in different colors every day.
Water
Don't forget to drink water. We should drink 6-8 glasses of water every day so that our bodies work well.

questions.prompt: How many kinds of dairy products are mentioned (提到) in the text (文本)?
questions.translation: 文中提到了多少种乳制品？
questions.explanation: 根据第二段 'such as milk, cheese, yogurt' 可知，文中提到了牛奶、奶酪和酸奶这三种乳制品。

questions.prompt: Which is a healthy balanced meal according to the passage?
options: Milk, fish, carrots, apples, rice & water.
Hamburgers, ice cream, tomatoes, bananas, rice & juice.
Beef, chicken, pizza, chocolate, rice & juice.
Soft drinks, oranges, meat, noodles, rice & water.
questions.translation: 根据文章，以下哪项是健康均衡的饮食？
questions.explanation: 根据文章，乳制品（牛奶）、鱼肉类、蔬菜水果（胡萝卜、苹果）、谷物（米饭）以及水都是文章推荐的健康食物，故A正确；B、C、D选项中包含了汉堡、冰淇淋、披萨、巧克力、软饮料等垃圾食品，不属于推荐 of 健康均衡饮食。

questions.prompt: What's the writer's opinion about eating well?
options: We need to eat much meat as we like every day.
We need to eat few grain products to keep fit.
Enough water can make our bodies very strong.
We should have different food and enough water.
questions.translation: 作者关于健康饮食的看法是什么？
questions.explanation: 通读全文，作者建议我们要吃多种不同类型的食物（different types of food）并喝足够的水（drink 6-8 glasses of water），对应D选项。

questions.prompt: In which part of a magazine can we probably read the text?
questions.translation: 我们可能在杂志的哪个版块读到这篇文章？
questions.explanation: 本文主要讨论如何通过合理饮食和饮水来保持身体健康，属于“健康（Health）”板块。

sections.title: 一、阅读理解 第一节 B
sections.instruction: 根据短文内容从各题的四个选项中选择一个最佳答案。
sections.passage:
Do you like reading stories? Do you read The Ugly Duckling or The Little Mermaid (《海的女儿》)? Do you know the writer of these great stories? It's Hans Christian Andersen, a Dane (丹麦人).
In real life, Andersen was like an ugly duckling. His early life is full of struggles. In 1805, Andersen was born in a poor family in Denmark. As a young boy, Andersen was tall, ugly and had a very big nose. His friends always laughed at him. After his father died in 1816, little Andersen needed to make money by himself.
When he was 17, he met a manager (经理) of a theatre. With the manager's help, Andersen went back to school. He had to study with little children. It was there that he began writing.
His first storybook became popular when it came out in 1835. The experiences of his life helped him write many wonderful stories. These stories could always win children's hearts. Children could feel his kindness and love in his stories. The poor ugly boy soon became famous, just like the ugly duckling turned into a beautiful swan. Today, children from different countries still love reading Andersen's stories.

questions.prompt: Why does the writer ask three questions at the beginning?
options: To tell us how to write stories.
To make us interested in the text.
To show us how to read more books.
To show us where to buy storybooks.
questions.translation: 为什么作者在开头问了三个问题？
questions.explanation: 在文章开头提出与读者熟悉的故事相关的问题，是为了引起读者的阅读兴趣，引出下文关于安徒生的介绍。

questions.prompt: What does the underlined word "struggles" in Paragraph 2 mean?
questions.translation: 第二段中画线单词 “struggles” 的意思是什么？
questions.explanation: 根据下文描述，安徒生出生在贫困家庭，长相不好被朋友嘲笑，父亲去世后还要自己挣钱，说明他的早期生活充满了“困难（Difficulties / 挣扎）”。

questions.prompt:
Which is the right order of the following events?
① Andersen's first book came out.
② Andersen's father died.
③ Andersen met a theatre manager.
④ Andersen went back to school and started writing.
questions.translation: 以下事件的正确顺序是什么？
questions.explanation: 父亲去世（1816年）-> 17岁时遇到剧院经理 -> 在经理的帮助下回到学校开始写作 -> 第一本书出版（1835年）。因此正确顺序为 ②③④①。

questions.prompt: What is the main idea of the last paragraph?
options: Anderson wrote many wonderful stories for parents.
Anderson's life experiences helped him write stories.
Anderson was like an ugly chicken who turned into a swan.
Anderson's stories won children's hearts and he became famous.
questions.translation: 最后一段的主要内容是什么？
questions.explanation: 最后一段主要说明安徒生的第一本书大受欢迎，他的生活经历帮助他写出了很多赢得孩子们喜爱的绝妙故事，并最终成名，故选D。

sections.title: 一、阅读理解 第一节 C
sections.instruction: 根据短文内容从各题的四个选项中选择一个最佳答案。
sections.passage:
Once there lived a poor shoemaker. He had enough leather (皮) for only one pair of shoes. One night, he cut out the pieces for the shoes and went to bed.
The next morning, the shoemaker found a pair of shoes on his table! They were really nice. He was very surprised. Later, a man came to buy the shoes. The shoemaker took the money and bought leather for two pairs of shoes. That night, he cut out more pieces and went to bed.
Again, he found shoes on his table in the morning! Then, he sold the shoes and bought more leather. That happened again and again, and soon the shoemaker wasn't poor.
One night, the shoemaker and his wife decided to stay up and see who was helping them. They hid (躲避) and waited. That night, two elves (小精灵) came and started to work on the table.
Next morning, the wife said, "The elves made us rich. We must thank them. So, I will make clothes and you will make shoes for them." The shoemaker loved that idea, and they started to work. Soon, everything was ready, and they put the things on the table. Then, they hid again.
The elves came at night. They found the clothes and shoes and they were very happy. They put everything on and danced happily out the door.

questions.prompt: Why did the shoemaker become rich?
options: The elves made shoes for him.
The elves gave him a lot of money.
He worked hard day and night.
His wife was good at selling shoes.
questions.translation: 为什么鞋匠变得富有了？
questions.explanation: 根据文章前半部分，小精灵在夜里帮鞋匠做鞋，鞋匠卖掉鞋买更多皮革，如此循环，鞋匠很快就富裕了。故选A。

questions.prompt: Which paragraph does the picture match? (Assuming the picture shows the shoemaker and his wife hiding and watching the elves)
questions.explanation: 第四段提到：'One night, the shoemaker and his wife decided to stay up and see who was helping them. They hid and waited. That night, two elves came and started to work on the table.' 对应鞋匠夫妇躲藏并看见小精灵工作的情景，故选C。

questions.prompt: What does the underlined word "they" in Paragraph 4 refer to (指代)?
questions.translation: 第四段中画线单词 “they” 指代的是什么？
questions.explanation: 在 'They hid and waited.' 句中，代词 they 指代前一句的主语 'the shoemaker and his wife'（鞋匠和他的妻子）。故选B。

questions.prompt: What is the best title (标题) for this text?
options: The Shoemaker and the Kind Elves
The Shoemaker and His Lucky Wife
questions.translation: 这篇文章最好的标题是什么？
questions.explanation: 本文讲述了鞋匠和默默帮助他的善良小精灵的故事，因此最适合的标题是《鞋匠和善良的小精灵》。故选C。

sections.title: 一、阅读理解 第二节
sections.instruction: 阅读短文，从所给的选项中选出可以填入空白处的最佳选项，其中有一个多余的选项。
sections.passage:
Weather changes all the time. Sometimes it's sunny; sometimes it's rainy. And the temperature changes every day too. [1]
Sunshine is good for our moods. Sunshine makes us think positively (积极地). [2] For example, on sunny weekends, many people choose to visit parks or take a walk in the countryside, smiling more and talking happily with their family and friends along the way.
[3] For example, cloudy or rainy days may make us feel sad or tired. Temperature can also change how we act. Some studies show that people may act more aggressively (挑衅地) in high temperatures.
Weather seems to have a great effect on our daily lives. But don't worry! We can control (控制) our feelings if we think positively. [4] After all, hope is always around the corner, and our thoughts can truly change how we feel.
wordbank: However, weather can make us feel bad, too.
Do you know weather can affect our feelings?
We can listen to the weather report before going out.
Next time when the weather's bad, tell yourself another good day is on its way.

questions.answer: Do you know weather can affect our feelings?
questions.explanation: 根据后文“阳光对我们的情绪有好处……”以及下文讨论的天气对人心情和行为的影响，这里引入主题“你知道天气会影响我们的情绪吗？”最合适。故选C。
空格前提到“阳光能让我们积极思考”，空格后举例说人们在晴朗的周末去公园散步、微笑和快乐地交谈。A选项“它也让我们快乐”正好承上启下，符合语境。故选A。

questions.answer: However, weather can make us feel bad, too.
questions.explanation: 空格后举例说阴天或雨天会让我们感到悲伤或疲倦，与上一段讨论晴天带来的好心情形成对比。B选项“然而，天气也会让我们感觉糟糕”作为本段主题句最合适。故选B。

questions.answer: Next time when the weather's bad, tell yourself another good day is on its way.
questions.explanation: 空格前提到“如果我们积极思考，就能控制自己的情绪”，空格后说“毕竟，希望总在拐角处”。E选项“下次天气不好时，告诉自己另一个好天就在路上了”符合这种积极的心态，是完美的建议。故选E。

sections.title: 二、完形填空
sections.instruction: 阅读短文，理解其大意，从各小题的四个选项中选择一个最佳答案。
sections.passage:
I was born in a small village. Every spring I saw how busy [1] were and what the season meant to them. Spring is a time when the [2] are ready for crops (庄稼). If you are [3] in spring, you will get nothing in autumn. So farmers work hard in spring.
When I was a little girl, every weekend I was busy planting crops. I had fun and [4] a lot. For example, when we planted potatoes, we needed to [5] the sprouted (发芽的) potatoes into pieces and made sure every piece of "seed" (种子) had one or two sprouts. It was really like a math game.
Farm work is also teamwork. That's why every spring field is busy with people of all ages working in it. While planting beans, my father dug small holes (坑), and I [6] put some seeds into each hole. The work was hard and [7], but when we worked together, it seemed easy and interesting.
I also felt the magic of [8] in spring. In a few weeks, the seeds sprouted. They seem to encourage us to [9] them by watering and so on. As time went by, they [10] into healthy plants. Because of these wonderful childhood experiences, I hold such a special feeling for spring.

questions.explanation: 后面提到“So farmers work hard in spring”，因此这里是指每到春天看到农民有多么忙碌。故选C。
春天是田地准备好种植庄稼的时候。故选A。
根据后文“秋天将一无所获”可知，如果在春天懒惰（lazy），就会没有收获。故选C。
后文提到“For example... It was really like a math game.”，说明作者在种植庄稼的过程中学到了很多。故选D。
根据常识和后文的 'into pieces'（成碎片），种土豆时需要把发芽的土豆切开。故选B。
父亲挖好洞，作者仔细地、认真地（carefully）把种子放进坑里。故选D。
空格后有 but 进行转折：“但是当我们一起工作时，它似乎变得轻松而有趣”。这说明原本工作很辛苦而且枯燥（boring）。故选A。
下文描述种子发芽并长成健康的植株，这体现的是春天里生命（life）的奥妙与魔力。故选B。
后文提到 'by watering and so on'（通过浇水等方式），这说明是照顾（take care of）它们。故选C。
随着时间的推移，它们长成了（grew into）健康的植物。故选D。

sections.title: 三、语篇填空
sections.instruction: 根据规则，现已将原本需要手工录入的语篇填空题转换为【下拉菜单单项选择题】，请从给出的选项中选出最契合上下文的正确答案形式。
sections.passage:
Rules are around us in our daily life. We need to follow the rules, but sometimes people have to break them for a special reason.
A team went to Antarctica to make a film about emperor penguins for [1] TV show. They watched the baby penguins come out of their [2] (egg) and grow up. That was [3] (real) amazing.
However, a big snowstorm came! The wind blew hard, and the snow [4] (fly) everywhere. The team worried about the baby penguins, but they couldn't do anything until (直到) the storm stopped.
[5] the storm, they went to see the baby penguins. To [6] (they) surprise, many baby penguins died. Some were still alive (活着) but they were trapped (被困) in big holes in the ice. The team [7] (feel) sad. The baby penguins were like family to them. They knew that people usually didn't interfere (干涉) with animals, [8] they couldn't just watch the baby penguins die. They decided [9] (give) a helping hand. They helped all the baby penguins get out of the holes. They were very excited to see the baby penguins safe and [10] (health). Even though some people might disagree, helping the penguins was the right thing to do in the filmmakers' eyes.

questions.explanation: TV show 是可数名词单数，首字母发音为辅音音素，在此表示泛指“一个”，用不定冠词 a。
企鹅宝宝们是从蛋里孵化出来的，且 baby penguins 是复数，对应的蛋也应用复数形式 eggs。
修饰形容词 amazing 需要使用副词 really，表示“真的很……”。
整篇故事以过去时叙述，fly 应用过去式 flew。
根据上下文，他们等到暴风雪停止了才去查看企鹅，因此是暴风雪“之后（After）”。注意首字母大写。
固定搭配 to one's surprise，此处指代“他们”，故用形容词性物主代词 their。
叙述过去发生的事，feel 应用过去式 felt。
前句“不干涉野生动物”与后句“不能看着它们死去”之间是转折关系，故填连词 but。
固定搭配 decide to do sth.（决定做某事），因此这里用动词不定式 to give。
and 连接并列成分，safe 是形容词，所以此处也应该填形容词 healthy，作宾语补足语。

sections.title: 四、阅读与表达
sections.instruction: 根据短文内容，完成下列各题（根据规则，前三题简答已改造为最佳单选项，第四题主观题升级为多项备选包容性考核模式）。
sections.passage:
Ben Yuechen is a boy with only one hand, but that didn't stop him from working a miracle (奇迹). Last year he set a world record—he did 21 burpees (波比跳) in one minute. His way to the world record was hard, but the boy never gave up.
When Ben was born, his family found he didn't have a left hand. They felt like it was the end of the world. When he was a young child, they seldom took him out. They were afraid that others' stares (凝视) would hurt him. They wanted to protect (保护) him by hiding him.
While Ben was in the first grade, his mother recorded a video of him learning to jump rope. After she shared the video online, many people showed their love and support for Ben. This gave the family hope. They wanted more people to see that Ben could do many things with one hand. From hiding to showing, the family learned that the best way to help a child grow is to let him experience the wind and rain.
Now Ben does different kinds of sports, such as burpee and badminton. Doing sports brings him benefits (益处), like keeping fit and having a strong heart. After setting the world record, the boy wants to keep trying and dreams of being a world champion (冠军) one day.

questions.prompt: What world record did Ben set last year?
options: He learned to jump rope with only one hand.
He did 21 burpees in one minute.
He became a world champion in badminton.
He did 100 pushups in one minute.
questions.translation: 本去年创造了什么世界纪录？
questions.explanation: 根据第一段第二句 'Last year he set a world record—he did 21 burpees (波比跳) in one minute.' 可知，他在一分钟内做了21个波比跳。

questions.prompt: According to Ben's family, what is the best way to help a child grow?
options: To protect them by hiding them at home.
To keep them away from any hard sports.
To let them experience the wind and rain.
To record videos and share them online.
questions.translation: 根据本的家人，帮助孩子成长最好的方式是什么？
questions.explanation: 根据第三段最后一句 '...the family learned that the best way to help a child grow is to let him experience the wind and rain.' 可知应选C。

questions.prompt: How can Ben become a world champion one day? (Select the most appropriate answer according to the passage)
options: He can keep trying and never give up.
He can stop doing sports and just stay at home.
He can ask his mother to post more videos.
He can wait for someone to give him another record.
questions.translation: 本怎样才能在未来某天成为世界冠军？
questions.explanation: 结合文章第一段“the boy never gave up”和最后一段“the boy wants to keep trying”，他需要继续努力且永不放弃，故选A。

questions.prompt: Please write something about your favorite sport and the benefits you get. (Select the statement that best matches the writing requirements)
options: My favourite sport is basketball. I like playing it with my classmates. It is very exciting.
My favourite sport is running. It keeps me healthy and makes me full of energy for my study every day.
I do not like any sports because I think doing sports is too tiring and boring.
Badminton. I play it every Saturday afternoon. It makes me happy.
questions.translation: 请写写你最喜欢的运动以及带给你的益处。（选出最符合写作要求的一项）
questions.explanation: 选项B句式完整，清晰表达了最喜欢的运动（running）以及其带来的益处（keeps healthy and makes full of energy），且字数符合30词左右的要求。A和D过于简短未达30词，C表达了不喜欢的消极观点。
